mission: Teach agents to write custom Matrix Core kernels with rocWMMA, inline mfma, Composable Kernel/CK Tile, and architecture-specific MFMA paths while treating hipBLASLt, Composable Kernel, Triton, MIGraphX, vLLM on ROCm, vLLM, and FlashInfer as baselines, correctness oracles, references, and extension surfaces.

must_not_claim: rocprofiler/rocprof counter evidence unless profiler artifacts are attached
fair FP32 comparison when TF32 Matrix Core math is enabled in the library baseline
fair FP8 comparison without scale, amax, accumulator, and output metadata
epilogue win unless hipBLASLt and Composable Kernel epilogue surfaces were considered

tasks.objective: Provide a CPU reference harness, scalar CUDA baseline, and simple rocWMMA 16x16x16 FP16-input FP32-accumulate custom kernel.
win_hypotheses: The simple rocWMMA path should beat the scalar HIP baseline for tile-aligned shapes.
hipBLASLt and Composable Kernel should usually beat the seed rocWMMA path until shared-memory staging, more output tiles per CTA, and stronger scheduling are added.
negative_examples_to_keep: One-warp-per-tile rocWMMA loses to Composable Kernel due to weak memory pipeline and low occupancy.
Ragged fallback is correct but slow because edge handling has not been tiled.

tasks.objective: Replace rocWMMA with inline mfma for a fixed GFX target and document register fragment layouts and ldmatrix shared-memory layout.
win_hypotheses: Inline MFMA can remove rocWMMA abstraction limits for fixed instruction shapes.
A bad register or shared-memory layout can lose despite using Matrix Cores.

tasks.objective: Separate strict FP32 and TF32 Matrix Core contracts so agents do not claim unfair wins or losses.
win_hypotheses: TF32 library baselines can dominate strict FP32 custom kernels when the math contract allows TF32.
Disabling TF32 changes both performance and correctness tolerance.

tasks.objective: Add a BF16 Matrix Core path with explicit conversion and tolerance metadata.
win_hypotheses: BF16 needs its own task because conversion and tolerance differ from FP16.
Composable Kernel/CK Tile may be the cleanest custom route when direct rocWMMA support is awkward for the target.

tasks.objective: Study FP8 GEMM through hipBLASLt and Composable Kernel/CK Tile before writing raw CUDA, with scale and amax metadata as first-class fields.
win_hypotheses: Custom wins may come from fixed scale layout, fused dequantization, fused quantization, or unsupported epilogues.
Plain FP8 GEMM throughput is likely hard to beat without reusing Composable Kernel/CK Tile architecture paths.

tasks.objective: Fuse bias, activation, residual, scale, or quantization after the Matrix Core mainloop and compare against hipBLASLt and Composable Kernel epilogues.
win_hypotheses: Unsupported or narrow fused epilogues can beat library paths that need extra launches or materialized intermediates.
hipBLASLt remains the strongest baseline when its epilogue exactly matches the task.

tasks.objective: Use Composable Kernel/CK Tile to study CDNA3 WGMFMA, global-to-LDS staging, clusters, and gfx950 portability before attempting raw CUDA.
win_hypotheses: CDNA3-specific kernels can win for fixed shapes or fused epilogues but require precise architecture metadata.
A portable gfx942 path and an gfx950 path should be recorded separately.

tasks.objective: Track CDNA4/RDNA4 Matrix Core and low-precision instruction paths with exact ROCm toolkit, GFX flag, and library-version metadata.
win_hypotheses: New low-precision paths should start from library baselines and Composable Kernel/CK Tile examples.
Custom wins are most plausible with fixed scale formats, fused epilogues, or deployment-specific layout constraints.
